topic_key.Arithmetic: Win on speed + accuracy: know your mental-math shortcuts, operation order, and fraction/percent fluency.
topic_key.Algebra: Translate words into symbols, isolate the unknown, and exploit structure (factoring, Vieta's, sequences).
topic_key.Geometry: Always draw it. Master the core angle/area/Pythagorean formulas and the length:area:volume scaling of similar figures.
topic_key.Number Theory: Think in primes, divisibility, and remainders (mod) — most problems reduce to one of these three lenses.
topic_key.Combinatorics: First decide: does order matter? Multiply independent choices; count the complement for 'at least'.
topic_key.Probability: Favorable ÷ total over a clearly-defined sample space; lean on complements and expected value.
topic_key.Statistics: Order the data first: median = middle, mode = most frequent, mean = average.

subtopic_key.arith.ops: When an expression MIXES operations (×/÷ with +/−) or has parentheses/exponents, apply PEMDAS: powers, then ×/÷, then +/−, left to right.
subtopic_key.arith.frac: Common denominator to add/subtract; '% of' = multiply by the decimal; move freely between fraction↔decimal↔percent.
subtopic_key.arith.ratio: Set up a proportion and cross-multiply; split a total by the sum of the ratio's parts.
subtopic_key.arith.rate: rate = amount ÷ time; convert units by multiplying conversion factors. Average speed = total distance ÷ total time (NOT the average of the speeds).
subtopic_key.arith.numlit: A SINGLE operation, a square, or a same-operation chain — reach for a mental shortcut (×11 add-neighbors, ×99 near-100, 25×4=100, squares via identity). No order-of-operations decision.
subtopic_key.arith.est: Round each factor to a friendly number, compute, and stay inside the accepted band.
subtopic_key.arith.roman: I=1, V=5, X=10, L=50, C=100, D=500, M=1000; a smaller symbol before a larger one subtracts (IV=4, XL=40).
subtopic_key.alg.linear: Undo operations in reverse to isolate x; flip the inequality sign when you multiply or divide by a negative.
subtopic_key.alg.systems: Eliminate or substitute — add/subtract the equations to cancel one variable.
subtopic_key.alg.quad: Factor or use the quadratic formula. Vieta's: sum of roots = −b/a, product = c/a.
subtopic_key.alg.poly: Remainder Theorem: the remainder of p(x)÷(x−a) is p(a); p(a)=0 ⇔ (x−a) is a factor.
subtopic_key.alg.exponents: aᵐ·aⁿ=aᵐ⁺ⁿ, (aᵐ)ⁿ=aᵐⁿ, a^(m/n)=ⁿ√(aᵐ); simplify radicals by pulling out perfect-square factors.
subtopic_key.alg.func: Composition is inside-out; for a custom operator just substitute into its definition.
subtopic_key.alg.seq: Arithmetic: aₙ=a₁+(n−1)d, sum = n·(first+last)/2. Geometric: aₙ=a₁·rⁿ⁻¹, sum = a₁(rⁿ−1)/(r−1).
subtopic_key.alg.log: log_b(x)=y ⇔ bʸ=x; log(xy)=log x+log y and log(x/y)=log x−log y.
subtopic_key.alg.absval: Split into two cases (inside positive / inside negative); the two roots of |ax+b|=c sum to −2b/a.
subtopic_key.alg.word: Name the unknown, turn each sentence into an equation, solve, then check against the wording.
subtopic_key.geom.angles: Triangle angles sum to 180°; an n-gon's interior angles sum to 180°(n−2); use parallel-line angle pairs.
subtopic_key.geom.triangles: Use congruence/similarity; isosceles base angles are equal; area = ½·base·height (or Heron's formula).
subtopic_key.geom.pyth: a²+b²=c². Memorize the triples 3-4-5, 5-12-13, 8-15-17, 7-24-25 and their multiples.
subtopic_key.geom.area: Rectangle l·w, triangle ½bh, circle πr²; surface area = sum of the faces; back out a side from the perimeter.
subtopic_key.geom.circles: C=2πr, A=πr²; arc & sector scale by (central angle)/360; an inscribed angle is half its central angle.
subtopic_key.geom.coord: Use the distance and midpoint formulas; slope = Δy/Δx; a line is y = mx + b.
subtopic_key.geom.solid: Volume: prism Bh, cylinder πr²h, cone ⅓πr²h, sphere 4/3πr³; surface area = sum of faces.
subtopic_key.geom.transform: Reflection negates one coordinate; a 90° CCW rotation sends (x,y)→(−y,x); translations add.
subtopic_key.geom.similar: In similar figures, lengths scale by k, areas by k², and volumes by k³.
subtopic_key.geom.trig: SOH-CAH-TOA for right triangles; Law of Cosines c²=a²+b²−2ab·cosC for the rest.
subtopic_key.nt.divis: Know the divisibility tests (2,3,4,5,9,11); divisible by both m and n ⇒ divisible by lcm(m,n).
subtopic_key.nt.primes: Break into prime factors first — the sum or count of prime factors then reads straight off.
subtopic_key.nt.gcdlcm: gcd × lcm = the product of the two numbers; or take min/max exponents from the prime factorizations.
subtopic_key.nt.mod: Work with remainders; powers repeat in a cycle — reduce the exponent modulo the cycle length.
subtopic_key.nt.divisors: # of divisors = product of (each exponent + 1); sum of divisors = product of (1+p+…+pᵉ).
subtopic_key.nt.bases: Place value: digit dₖ in base b is worth dₖ·bᵏ; convert by repeated division and reading remainders.
subtopic_key.nt.digit: Write a two-digit number as 10a+b, then translate the digit condition into an equation.
subtopic_key.nt.dioph: Integer solutions only — use mod/divisibility to bound, or factor (e.g. a²−b²=(a−b)(a+b)) and enumerate.
subtopic_key.nt.pattern: Lean on parity and invariants; sum of the first n odd numbers = n²; hunt for a repeating cycle.
subtopic_key.comb.fund: Multiply the number of independent choices (the multiplication principle).
subtopic_key.comb.perm: Order matters: n! total arrangements, P(n,k)=n!/(n−k)!, circular = (n−1)!.
subtopic_key.comb.case: Break into disjoint cases, or count the complement (total − unwanted) — especially for 'at least'.
subtopic_key.comb.pascal: The xⁿ⁻ᵏyᵏ term of (x+y)ⁿ is C(n,k); row n of Pascal's triangle sums to 2ⁿ.
subtopic_key.comb.pigeonhole: More items than boxes ⇒ some box holds ≥2; find the worst case and add 1.
subtopic_key.comb.grid: Right/up lattice paths = C(total steps, # rights); through a waypoint, multiply the two leg counts.
subtopic_key.prob.basic: P = favorable ÷ total over an equally-likely sample space.
subtopic_key.prob.compound: Independent events multiply; 'or' adds (then subtract the overlap).
subtopic_key.prob.cond: A condition shrinks the sample space — recount favorable and total after the given info.
subtopic_key.prob.ev: Expected value = Σ(value × probability) — weight every outcome by its chance.
subtopic_key.prob.count: Build the numerator and denominator with combinations/permutations, then divide.
subtopic_key.stat.median: Sort the data; the median is the middle value (average the two middle ones if the count is even).
subtopic_key.stat.mode: The mode is the value appearing most often (and the mean is sum ÷ count).
subtopic_key.stat.mean: The mean is sum ÷ count; unlike the median it gets pulled toward outliers.
